questions.text: What was the term used for big landowners or warrior chiefs in the 7th century?
questions.explanation: Kings often acknowledged warrior chiefs or big landowners as their subordinates or Samantas.

options: Become a 'kshatriya' even if not one by birth
questions.explanation: Literally meaning the 'golden womb', this ritual was thought to lead to the rebirth of the sacrificer as a Kshatriya.

questions.text: Which dynasty was involved in the 'Tripartite Struggle' for Kanauj?
questions.explanation: For centuries, rulers belonging to these three dynasties fought for control over Kanauj.

questions.text: Who wrote 'Rajatarangini', a history of the kings of Kashmir?
questions.explanation: Kalhana used a variety of sources, including inscriptions and documents, to write his Sanskrit poem.

questions.text: Sultan Mahmud of Ghazni was a ruler from which country?
questions.explanation: He ruled Ghazni in Afghanistan and raided the Indian subcontinent 17 times.

questions.text: The Chola king who was known for creating a powerful navy was:
questions.explanation: Rajendra I expanded the empire and even raided Southeast Asian countries using his navy.

questions.text: Settlements of peasants under the Cholas were known as:
questions.explanation: Settlements of peasants became prosperous with the spread of irrigation agriculture and were called Ur.

questions.text: Land gifted to Brahmanas was known as:
questions.explanation: Brahmadeya was land granted to Brahmanas, often recorded on copper plates.

questions.text: The assembly of prominent Brahmana landholders was called:
questions.explanation: The Sabha looked after the affairs of the Brahmadeya settlements.

questions.text: Who founded the Chola empire by capturing the Kaveri delta from the Muttaraiyar?
questions.explanation: Vijayalaya, who belonged to the ancient family of the Cholas from Uraiyur, captured the delta in the 9th century.
